title: Preparation method of liraglutide derivative
abstract: Disclosed herein is a method for method of producing a liraglutide derivative of formula (I), in which, R i is tert -butyl methyl or benzyl, and R 2 is a C 12-18 acyl. The method includes steps of, (a) coupling a liraglutide precursor with a compound of formula (II) in a solvent system to produce the liraglutide derivative of formula (I), in which the coupling occurs at the lysine residue at position 26 of the liraglutide precursor via forming an amide linkage; (b) quenching the reaction of the step (a) with an addition of a glycine solution; and (c) collecting the liraglutide derivative of formula (I) from the quenched solution of the step (b); wherein, the solvent system is a mixture of water, a water-miscible solvent and an alcohol mixed in a volume ratio from about 1-5:1-5:1-5; the liraglutide precursor is Arg- 34 Lys 26 -GLP-1(7-37)-OH; and the compound of formula (II) has the structure of, wherein R i is tert -butyl, methyl or benzyl; and R 2 is a C 12-18 acyl.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method of producing a liraglutide derivative of formula (I), and the method comprises: (a) coupling a liraglutide precursor with a compound of formula (II) in a solvent system to produce the liraglutide derivative of formula (I), in which the coupling occurs at the lysine residue at position 26 of the liraglutide precursor via forming an amide linkage; (b) quenching the reaction of the step (a) with an addition of a glycine solution; and (c) collecting the liraglutide derivative of formula (I) from the quenched solution of the step (b); wherein, the solvent system is a mixture of water, a water-miscible solvent and an alcohol mixed in a volume ratio from about 1-5: 1-5: 1-5; the liraglutide precursor is Arg- 34 Lys 26 -GLP-l(7-37)-OH; and the compound of formula (II) has the structure of, wherein Ri is tert- butyl, methyl or benzyl; and R 2 is a C12-18 acyl. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein in the step (a), the water-miscible solvent is selected from the group consisting of acetonitrile, dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, tetrahydrofuran, and a combination thereof; and the alcohol is selected from the group consisting of methanol, ethanol, propanol, isopropanol, Â«-butanol, and a combination thereof. 3. The method of claim 2, wherein the solvent system is a mixture of water, N-methyl-2- pyrrolidone and methanol in the volume ratio of 1 : 1 : 1. 4. The method of claim 2, further comprising, (b-l) adjusting the pH value of the quenched solution of the step (b) to about 4.0 to 6.5. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the pH value is adjusted by the addition of trifluoroacetic acid (TFA). 6. The method of claim 4, further comprising, (b-2) adding an anti-solvent to the quenched solution of the step (b-l) to precipitate the liraglutide derivative of formula (I) therefrom. 7. The method of claim 6, wherein the anti-solvent is selected from the group consisting of water, ether, methyl tert- buty ether and a combination thereof. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the anti-solvent is water. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein in the compound of formula (II), R 2 is selected from the group consisting of stearioyl, palmitoyl, myristoyl, and laurioyl; and Ri is tert- butyl, methyl or benzyl. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein in the compound of formula (II), Ri is tert-butyl, and R 2 is palmitoyl. 11. The method of claim 1, wherein in the step (a), the compound of formula (II) is about 2-10 folds to that of the liraglutide precursor. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the amount of the compound of formula (II) is about 5-folds to that of the liraglutide precursor.
